title: Create an Event and Registration System

content.problemStatement:
In this practice problem, you will create an Event & Registration System using Django's model field types and field options.  \
The project, url mapping & addition & retrieval of data via views are all completed for you.
#### Your Task:
In the `models.py` file, define the following two models with the specified fields, field types, and options: 
#### Model 1: Event
| Field Name  | Field Type   | Options                                                      |
| ----------- | ------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------ |
| name        | CharField    | `max_length=100`, `unique=True`, `verbose_name="Event Name"` |
| description | TextField    | `blank=True`                                                 |
| fee         | FloatField   | `default=0.0`, `null=True`                                   |
| is\_online  | BooleanField | `default=False`                                              |


#### Model 2: Registration
| Field Name          | Field Type    | Options                                                                |
| ------------------- | ------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| event               | ForeignKey    | `to=Event`, `on_delete=models.CASCADE`, `related_name="registrations"` |
| participant\_name   | CharField     | `max_length=100`, `verbose_name="Participant Name"`                    |
| participant\_email  | EmailField    | `null=True`                                                            |
| number\_of\_tickets | IntegerField  | `default=1`                                                            |
| registered\_at      | DateTimeField | `null=True`, `verbose_name="Registered At"`                            |

After completing the models, click on **RUN** which will run migrations and start the server. The result will be rendered via  homepage.html page whose output should look like:
